original_assumption: Printing can be treated as one workflow
current_lesson: Commercial print labels cartons corrugated and flexible packaging have materially different estimating converting and compliance paths
decision_effect: Choose one product family before interview or prototype

original_assumption: A stage board alone is enough
current_lesson: Approved specification artwork version material and quantity identity must precede useful stage status
decision_effect: Begin quote-to-job research at intake not dashboard layout

original_assumption: Live tracking is the obvious first feature
current_lesson: Specific exceptions POD quality and billing readiness may be more actionable for a small operator
decision_effect: Test exception and POD flow before route optimization or continuous tracking
source_or_method: Official digital logistics context plus process analysis

original_assumption: More driver data always improves operations
current_lesson: Driver updates can add distraction surveillance and adoption risk
decision_effect: Use minimum low-tap safe updates and explicit purpose

current_lesson: Revision sample approval material and cutting gates determine whether sewing can start successfully
decision_effect: Test style readiness before detailed line analytics

original_assumption: Worker-level efficiency should be an early metric
current_lesson: Worker surveillance is high risk and unnecessary for validating order-level coordination
decision_effect: Keep Phase 2 at style order blocker and quality-event level

original_assumption: SME manufacturing is a single target market
current_lesson: Production strategy product revision and route change the workflow more than firm-size label alone
decision_effect: Name a product family and process before using the manual

original_assumption: An ERP replacement is needed to create value
current_lesson: A readiness or hold worklist may operate beside current records and test evidence with lower risk
decision_effect: Avoid MRP costing and OEE claims in first pilot

original_assumption: A stock number is sufficient for reorder software
current_lesson: Product identity unit location and transaction timing must reconcile before recommendations are trustworthy
decision_effect: Validate twenty physical SKUs before proposing reorder logic
source_or_method: GS1 India guidance and workflow analysis

original_assumption: Sales growth is the primary retail software outcome
current_lesson: Dead stock stockout shrink discount return credit and working capital can erase gross margin
decision_effect: Ask category-level cash and exception questions without requesting confidential totals
